highlights.title: Agentic coding without the cloud: evaluating open-weight large language models on longitudinal data preparation tasks
highlights.summary: Open-weight LLMs enable local data preparation.
highlights.detail: This approach addresses governance requirements that prohibit data transmission to external services, making it suitable for research using personal data. The framework includes a curated dataset and automated evaluation routines.

highlights.title: PATS: Policy-Aware Training Scaffolding for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
highlights.summary: PATS reframes skills as dynamic training scaffolds.
highlights.detail: PATS converts rollout groups into evidence cards and adjusts context for subsequent rollouts, providing concrete guidance for weak policies. This approach improves exploration and policy optimization.

highlights.title: KroQuant: Kronecker-Structured Block Transforms for Efficient Post-Training Quantization of Diffusion Transformers
highlights.detail: KroQuant addresses the issue of outliers in activations, which cannot be represented by 4-bit formats. It applies an invertible linear transform to activations and weights, reducing inference computation cost.

highlights.title: Bringing Nunchaku 4-bit Diffusion Inference to Diffusers
highlights.detail: This release brings Nunchaku 4-bit diffusion inference to the Diffusers library, enabling efficient and accurate inference.

highlights.title: Show HN: Echo – Fable-level results at 1/3 the cost using open-weight models
highlights.summary: Echo combines open-weight models for better results.
highlights.detail: Echo is an experiment in combining open-weight models to achieve better results than individual models. It measures the performance of a hypothetical system that knows which models are useful for each task.

items.summary: Open-weight LLMs enable local data preparation.
items.detail: This approach addresses governance requirements that prohibit data transmission to external services, making it suitable for research using personal data. The framework includes a curated dataset and automated evaluation routines.

items.title: PATS: Policy-Aware Training Scaffolding for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: PATS reframes skills as dynamic training scaffolds.
items.detail: PATS converts rollout groups into evidence cards and adjusts context for subsequent rollouts, providing concrete guidance for weak policies. This approach improves exploration and policy optimization.

items.title: KroQuant: Kronecker-Structured Block Transforms for Efficient Post-Training Quantization of Diffusion Transformers
items.detail: KroQuant addresses the issue of outliers in activations, which cannot be represented by 4-bit formats. It applies an invertible linear transform to activations and weights, reducing inference computation cost.

items.title: Bringing Nunchaku 4-bit Diffusion Inference to Diffusers
items.detail: This release brings Nunchaku 4-bit diffusion inference to the Diffusers library, enabling efficient and accurate inference.

items.title: Show HN: Echo – Fable-level results at 1/3 the cost using open-weight models
items.summary: Echo combines open-weight models for better results.
items.detail: Echo is an experiment in combining open-weight models to achieve better results than individual models. It measures the performance of a hypothetical system that knows which models are useful for each task.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic unite against open-weight AI risks to their bottom line
items.summary: OpenAI and Anthropic unite against open-weight AI risks.
items.detail: This alliance aims to address the risks associated with open-weight AI, which could potentially disrupt their business models.

items.title: Jul 22, 2026 Product Ask Claude about the Anthropic Economic Index
items.detail: The Anthropic Economic Index is a new feature that provides insights into the economy. It is available for users to ask questions about.

items.title: Launching Health in ChatGPT
items.detail: The Health feature in ChatGPT allows eligible U.S. users to securely connect their medical records and Apple Health data to receive personalized insights and better understand their health.

items.title: Model: Motif-Technologies/Motif-3-Beta
items.detail: The Motif-3-Beta model is a text-generation model that has gained popularity, with over 1,800 downloads and 175 likes.

items.title: Model: Nanbeige/Nanbeige4.2-3B
items.detail: The Nanbeige4.2-3B model is a text-generation model that has gained significant attention, with over 4,500 downloads and 330 likes.

items.title: Mourning Dan Williams
items.detail: Dan Williams, a longtime kernel developer, has passed away. He was a strong presence in the Linux community and will be deeply missed.

items.title: Security updates for Thursday
items.detail: Security updates have been issued for various Linux distributions, including AlmaLinux, Debian, Fedora, SUSE, and Ubuntu. These updates address several vulnerabilities and provide important security patches.

items.title: [$] An operations structure for swap devices
items.summary: Linux swap subsystem to get operations structure
items.detail: The swap subsystem's evolution led to a lack of abstraction, which will be addressed with an operations structure. This change aims to improve the interface between the swap layer and devices.

items.title: Codeberg: Protecting our FLOSS commons from LLMs
items.detail: Codeberg's new policy aims to promote human collaboration and prevent the hosting of single-use software generated by LLMs, focusing on improving existing software together.

items.title: A Train Departure Board For The Home
items.detail: A project to create a train departure board for personal use, helping individuals stay on schedule.

items.title: BASICally, Its Retro Machine Language
items.detail: A look back at a BASIC game, The Wizard's Castle, written for the Exidy Sorcerer, showcasing retro machine language programming.

items.title: US Government targets Cop City protester over phone operating system
items.summary: US Government targets Cop City protester
items.detail: The US Government is targeting a Cop City protester over their phone's operating system, raising concerns about surveillance and privacy.

items.title: GLP: A Grassroots, Multiagent, Concurrent, Logic Programming Language for AI
items.summary: GLP: A grassroots logic programming language
items.detail: GLP is designed for implementing grassroots platforms, offering a multiagent concurrent logic programming language for egalitarian and democratic systems.

items.title: The Consensus Number of Untraceable Cryptocurrencies
items.detail: The choice between classical and constant-state schemes affects synchronization in untraceable cryptocurrencies, with LUAT having a consensus number of 2.

items.title: Top-down = Bottom-up: Sound and Complete Characterisations of Liveness by Multiparty Global Protocols
items.detail: Both top-down and bottom-up methodologies ensure liveness in multiparty session types, with the top-down strategy generating local types from a global type and the bottom-up strategy checking local types for liveness.

items.title: Imprecise Probabilistic Programming, Precisely: Credal Sets via Graded Monads, BDDs, and Semiring-Parametric Inference (Functional Pearl)
items.detail: The Imp language generalizes standard probability theory using convex sets of possible distributions, and it can be used with BDD compilation and weighted model counting.

items.title: Multimmit: Extending Blocks for Faster Finality
items.summary: Multimmit: Extending blocks for faster finality
items.detail: Multimmit improves upon existing blockchain protocols by allowing for faster finality and increased throughput, addressing the trade-off between certified and uncertified approaches.

items.title: alibaba/open-code-review
items.detail: The open-code-review tool uses a hybrid architecture with deterministic pipelines and an LLM agent, providing precise line-level comments and built-in rules for code review.

items.title: facebook/folly
items.detail: The folly library is a collection of reusable C++ components developed and used at Facebook, providing a range of functionality for building scalable and efficient systems.

items.title: fujiapple852/trippy
items.summary: Trippy is a network diagnostic tool
items.detail: It is written in Rust and available on GitHub, providing a simple way to diagnose network issues.

items.title: google/flatbuffers
items.summary: FlatBuffers is a memory efficient serialization library
items.detail: It is written in C++ and designed for high-performance applications, providing a compact binary format for data serialization.

items.title: raullenchai/Rapid-MLX
items.summary: Rapid-MLX is a local AI engine for Apple Silicon, 4.2x faster than Ollama
items.detail: It supports prompt cache, reasoning separation, and cloud routing, making it a drop-in replacement for OpenAI.

items.title: t8y2/dbx
items.summary: Dbx is a 20 MB lightweight cross-platform database client
items.detail: It supports various databases, including MySQL and PostgreSQL, and provides a built-in AI assistant and MCP Server.
